_comment: WC2026 Group H, Matchday 2 (Hard Rock Stadium, Miami). MD1: Uruguay 1-1 Saudi Arabia (poor, Nunez missed chances); Cape Verde 0-0 SPAIN (extremely tough low block, kept a clean sheet against Spain). bet365 odds Uruguay 1.478 / Draw 4.0 / CV 6.5 (Kalshi 68/23/11, Opta 67). Calibrated in line with the market, with draw/Under raised due to the DEMONSTRATED resilience of the Cape Verdean block. REFEREE/XI not confirmed -> cards placeholder. Araujo (Uruguay CB) doubtful.

meta.stakes: Group H. Uruguay (Bielsa, Nunez, Valverde) favorite but came in poor (1-1 Saudi Arabia); Cape Verde, a debutant, comes off a historic 0-0 against Spain and sits deep. A win sets up qualification.

home.name: Uruguay
notes: Bielsa, Nunez, Valverde: potent attack BUT blunt in MD1 (1-1 Saudi Arabia, Nunez missed). Faces the block that kept Spain scoreless -> attack 1.36 (not inflated).
Calibrated in line with the market (~63%), with elevated draw risk (Cape Verde already proved it can hold off a top team).

away.name: Cape Verde
notes: VERY organized low block: kept a clean sheet against Spain -> defense 1.06 (better than being a debutant would suggest).
Minimal attack (didn't score against Spain) -> attack 0.60.
Sits deep: cagey match with few goals -> rho -0.07.

cards._referee: REFEREE NOT YET CONFIRMED. Generic placeholder. Recalibrate once known.

_postmatch.result: Uruguay 2-2 Cape Verde (CV equalized twice; Uruguay nearly won it at the end). Open, tight match.
_postmatch.verdict: Favorite frustrated AGAIN by Cape Verde (debutant's 3rd point, after the 0-0 against Spain). The 1X2 missed (the draw 24.6% came in, within range).
_postmatch.lesson_1: I underestimated Cape Verde's attack (lambda 0.60; they scored 2). 'Low block' is NOT synonymous with 'harmless': when the match opens up and the favorite stretches, they DO create and convert (including a screamer). Their xG-for depends on the game state, it's not fixed at ~0.
_postmatch.lesson_2: The correct thesis for 'favorite frustrated by a low block' is the DRAW / double chance, NOT the Under/BTTS-No. Here I nailed the draw as a live scenario but recommended Under 2.5 (60%) and BTTS No (62%) -> BOTH LOST (2-2 = Over 2.5 and BTTS Yes). The robust vehicle for that thesis is the result (draw), not low goals.
_postmatch.model_adjustment: For future low blocks that have already shown bite (Cape Verde), raise their attack ~0.6->0.8 if the match projects open, and express the thesis via draw/double-chance instead of Under.
